pattern: Only cards holding the key letter matter, and they split into two clean families whose pairings reduce to a max-matching where same-letter cards cannot pair, solved by a simple counting formula plus a split of the doubled-letter cards.
hints: Discard every card that does not contain the key letter; the rest are exactly the ones in play.

Cards with the key letter in the first slot can only pair with each other (and with the doubled-letter card), and likewise for the second slot. Two cards sharing the same other letter are identical and cannot pair.

In each family the max number of pairs is min(total // 2, total - largest_group). The doubled-letter cards are a wildcard group you can assign to either family, so try every split of them and keep the best total.

edge_inputs.description: key letter never yields a compatible pair
identical doubled cards cannot pair with each other
no card contains the key letter
first-slot family with one dominating letter

stress.description: many two-letter cards over a small alphabet so both families and wildcards are dense
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    letters = 'abcdef'
    cards = [rng.choice(letters) + rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(n)]
    x = rng.choice(letters)
    return [cards, x]
stress.note: small alphabet packs the matching groups

oracle_python:
import heapq
class Solution:
    def score(self, cards, x):
        from collections import Counter
        cnt = Counter(cards)
        xx = cnt.get(x + x, 0)
        A = Counter()
        B = Counter()
        for c, n in cnt.items():
            if x not in c or c == x + x:
                continue
            if c[0] == x:
                A[c[1]] += n
            elif c[1] == x:
                B[c[0]] += n
        def greedy(groups):
            h = [-g for g in groups if g > 0]
            heapq.heapify(h)
            pairs = 0
            while len(h) >= 2:
                a = -heapq.heappop(h)
                b = -heapq.heappop(h)
                pairs += 1
                a -= 1
                b -= 1
                if a > 0:
                    heapq.heappush(h, -a)
                if b > 0:
                    heapq.heappush(h, -b)
            return pairs
        va = list(A.values())
        vb = list(B.values())
        best = 0
        for split in range(xx + 1):
            ga = va + ([split] if split > 0 else [])
            gb = vb + ([xx - split] if xx - split > 0 else [])
            best = max(best, greedy(ga) + greedy(gb))
        return best
